title: Xeraea haenkeana
intro: Xeraea haenkeana, also known as Anacyclus pyrethrum or Anacyclus pyrethrum subsp. haenkeanus, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including insomnia, digestive issues, and pain relief, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, sedative, and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Xeraea haenkeana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as to create essential oil blends and dried flower arrangements for its calming and soothing effects.
taxonomy: Xeraea haenkeana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Xeraea and is a member of the order Asterales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its position within the botanical hierarchy. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Xeraea haenkeana is closely related to other daisy-like plants. The classification provides valuable information for botanists and researchers studying this species.
uses: Xeraea haenkeana is a species of plant native to the Galapagos Islands, but unfortunately, there's limited information available on its medicinal uses. properties: Xeraea haenkeana has been reported to have potential therapeutic properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
